title: Read and discuss my thesis on Evangelion and Feminism
display_title: Read and discuss my thesis on Evangelion and Feminism
wikitext:
[]: Read and discuss my thesis on Evangelion and Feminism Post by peterw5 » Mon Oct 31, 2011 2:58 pm Hello everyone. I've been a longtime lurker on this forum and enjoyed reading all the discussions on Evangelion. I wrote my college thesis on Eva and would like to share it with you. you can read it on Google Docs here: [url]https://docs.google.com/document/d/18og9OJ1dp7s56JzsXROmbbZ0Q-TmYlwU83h9nRvoM98/edit[/url]

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Oct 31, 2011 4:53 pm You can't upload the text in a more ... normal context than a random download site file? I mean... how about pastebin, for example? Or practically anything else that lets one with a click read it in their browser instead of ... oh nevermind. Also maybe you could include your opening summation or something here, I dunno. I don't think many are inclined to just download this file with little more enticement than 'read this'.

[]: Post by peterw5 » Mon Oct 31, 2011 5:15 pm Well, I'd like to make it easier for people to look at, but I don't know how. Anybody want to tell me how?

[]: Post by gatotsu911 » Mon Oct 31, 2011 5:33 pm Skimmed it. Seems interesting; the topic is certainly one worthy of discussion. I see you went for the more "practical" route (the show's subversion of gender roles) as opposed to some of the more abstract aspects of the show's feminist ideas, such as its mysticism surrounding the power of the feminine.

[]: Post by Ornette » Mon Oct 31, 2011 5:41 pm Well, it's like 18 pages, including links, formatting, blockquotes and a bibliography. It would be a complete mess on pastebin unless you reformatted everything to look the same as in the Word document. You could try to print the doc into a postscript file so it can easily be opened on any computer. But unless you can host it somewhere, people are still going to need to go to a file serve site like mediafire. Does your school give you webspace on their servers?

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 31, 2011 6:09 pm Ornette wrote: But unless you can host it somewhere

[]: Post by honsou » Mon Oct 31, 2011 6:27 pm Academically speaking there are way too many colloquialisms. Best example off the top of my head is "She’s giving the bird to patriarchy". There are a lot of other issues too, such as "Misato gets sexually harassed by Kaji and another man at work". Who is the other guy? And in that same paragraph, tying together Mistato and Ritsuko's sexual relationships and homophobia in western society. And than there are just some lines that make your entire paper look like one giant forum posts with citations. Best example is this "He will do any immoral thing to get her back, even though he doesn’t deserve it. It’s pathetic. He doesn’t love her; he loves how she makes him feel." You just dont write like that in an academic paper, it just makes you look small. Speaking as a geek its...relatively clear you've never actually seen much of Gundam. As most of the series do have three dimensional characters and well developed plot points. Obviously this is more of just nerd rage but it was one of the first things I noticed.

[]: Post by peterw5 » Mon Oct 31, 2011 6:30 pm Thanks Mr. Tines (and others who tried to help a technophobe like me). I put it on Google Docs which was very easy to figure out how to use and seems like the most convenient place for everyone. @ honsou Thanks for the feedback. You're probably right about it being pretty amateur in regards to academic writing technique. I've never written a thesis other than this one, and It's just an undergrad thesis. (For what it's worth, I did get a good grade on it, but maybe the professor just gives good grades to everyone, regardless of quality, so that people will like him. Wouldn't surprise me.)

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Mon Oct 31, 2011 6:51 pm Ah, cool thanks for putting it on google docs like that. In some perusing I noticed that these gender relations explored are all through the lens of western/American experience, which is fine I guess. but academically speaking I'd think the fact that it's a Japanese show aimed at a Japanese audience would mean at least some, if not a majority of, the perspective should include comment about how these things are in the context of Japanese culture. Also, I gotta know... who is the other guy that harassed Misato at work? You can't possibly mean Hyuga, can you?

[]: Post by honsou » Mon Oct 31, 2011 7:00 pm @peterw5 It does take a little bit of work to get that kind of stuff right, as you basically need to change the way you speak while writing those papers to the point where you are in danger of losing your "voice" while writing these papers. I think a good rule is basically if you had to call it a "figure of speech" you shouldn't use it in this kind of paper.

[]: Post by Allemann » Mon Oct 31, 2011 7:04 pm For what course was this paper written?

[]: Post by peterw5 » Mon Oct 31, 2011 7:07 pm The other guy that "harasses" (perhaps that's too strong a word) Misato is Hyuga. He makes little passes at her, and obviously has a crush. @ Allemann It was my Senior thesis project. At the school I went to (I've graduated now), in the department I was in, it was a requirement to write a Senior thesis. One of the options was to write the thesis on a topic of your own choosing. So basically it's more of an independent research project than something that is done for a specific course.

[]: Post by Trajan » Mon Oct 31, 2011 7:23 pm lol so being lovestruck towards someone is now considered sexual harassment. When will the madness end?

[]: Post by peterw5 » Mon Oct 31, 2011 7:28 pm @ Trajan Being lovestruck isn't the problem. Making passes at coworkers is.

[]: Post by Trajan » Mon Oct 31, 2011 7:30 pm peterw5 wrote: Being lovestruck isn't the problem. Making passes at coworkers is.

[]: Post by Allemann » Mon Oct 31, 2011 7:31 pm peterw5 wrote: Making passes at coworkers is.

[]: Post by peterw5 » Mon Oct 31, 2011 7:45 pm I admit, it's a bit of a stretch to call what Hyuga does harrassment, and Misato is obviously not too phased by it. However, what he did could make certain people uncomfortable. Misato could have complained about it. Let's say that Hyuga made passes not at Misato, but at Aoba. I doubt that Aoba would have appreciated that. Now, if Misato and Aoba weren't coworkers, then no big deal, but since they are coworkers it is a big deal.

[]: Post by symbv » Mon Oct 31, 2011 8:06 pm In which episode did Hyuga make passes at Misato?

[]: Post by Sachi » Mon Oct 31, 2011 8:09 pm Well, it's official: Ryoji Kaji is a sexual predator! Let's stick a blue dot over his house and call it a day! Yes, because then progress can be made in the name of sexual equality! I like to think of myself as a problem solver; it's what I do.

[]: Post by Final Messenger » Mon Oct 31, 2011 8:10 pm One could argue that hyuga's "dying beside you is not a bad way to go" line could be making a pass all though one would have to elaborate a lot to make that plasuable
